And you're also well on your way to be able to close anyone who's closeable, which is what the straight line's all about. So it's really all about practice and repetition at this point, right? You want to make everything you've learned so far basically like locked in type by rinsing and repeating, rinsing and repeating, rinsing and repeating until really it can do it in your sleep at the highest possible level. In other words, through practice and repetition, you become basically unconsciously competent as the phrase goes and applying everything you've learned so far. And by doing that, you literally ensure your own success. Do you agree with that? Yes or no? In other words, through practice and repetition right now, you essentially get so great at what you've learned that it's almost an insurance policy to your own success. Yes or no? Well, unfortunately, while it might seem that way, it's actually not 100%. I mean, you are no doubt 95% of the way there, but you're not all the way there. And frankly, it's that 5% that kills you every single time. Okay, well, you've answered correctly, but I hope it's for the right reasons. You see, you are in fact 95% of the way there, but you're not all the way there. And frankly, or unfortunately, it's that 5% that ends up killing you every single time. What I'm driving at here is that if you don't have a strategy in place to ensure that you actually keep these new sales you've just made, meaning they don't end up canceling right after your closing or almost just as toxic, if you don't have a strategy or program in place for turning these first time buyers into repeat customers, then you're setting yourself up for having a really difficult time of it. I mean, listen, here's the deal. If you have the slightest bit of experience in either business or just in sales, then you've got to be aware. In fact, you've got to be painfully aware of how much it costs to actually land a new customer and how much time and energy it takes along the way, right? For example, first you have a developer marketing campaign that actually converts, then you have to perfect the handoff to the sales force, which is easier said than done sometimes. And once you have that under control, you have salesmen who have to get to the gatekeepers, to the actual decision makers and capture their attention in those all important first four seconds, right? Then you're going to get through the intelligence gathering session and then, just don't be scared here. I'm just illustrating the point. It's all very doable with the straight line system. And then if you're using more than a one call system, you have to repeat this whole process again and maybe you get into three call system because you've got to get in touch with that same person twice or three times, which isn't always so easy to do. And that was a mouthful. But here's my point. All that stuff that has to happen for you to shot of closing sale is typically the most expensive part of any company's business. And in many cases, it's what ends up putting them out of business. So given that reality, just imagine how destructive this is. If after you've finally got to make your presentation and the prospect says, yes, you either do something or don't do something that caused them to cancel the purchase or not quite as bad, but still bad enough, by the way. They get so turned off by the post closing experience, it's like you never buy from your company again. They become a one time buyer. You get it? So that's what this module is all about. It's about providing you with a set of very powerful strategies, which together will dramatically reduce any bleeding you're currently experiencing in the form of either customer churn or flat out cancellations, while also putting you in the perfect pole position to start selling this new customer additional products and ultimately become their supplier of first choice for whatever it is you're selling. Sound good? I'm sure it does. I mean, this stuff is crucial on every strategy I'm going to teach you here. I've used myself out in the field in the real world with great effectiveness. So I can promise you that it all works. See, like the rest of the straight line system is nothing theoretical here. So with that in mind, there are basically four things we're going to cover in this module. First, we'll go over exactly what you need to do immediately after the sale closes to essentially lock in this new customer so they don't cancel. That's number one. Number two, I'm going to show you how to step by step, take this new customer and get them to buy a second product or service from you, which turns them out into an actual client. Before that, they're just a customer. Number three, I'm going to walk you through a series of interlocking strategies. They'll be using this part of your everyday sales process to ensure that you do in fact end up creating those all important customers for life. And four, lastly, I'm going to teach you a seven step strategy that will allow you to build a massive referral business, which is so crucial to your long term success, especially when it comes down to bringing down your average lead cost, right? Because referrals are free and also, by the way, it will substantially reduce your daily stress level as referrals are frankly much easier to close and basically a pleasure to work with. All right?
